title: Ensure that the --audit-log-path argument is set

input_row.title: Ensure that the --audit-log-path argument is set
input_row.description: Enable auditing on the Kubernetes API Server and set the desired audit log path.
input_row.rationale: Auditing the Kubernetes API Server provides a security-relevant chronological set of records documenting the sequence of activities that have affected system by individual users, administrators or other components of the system. Even though currently, Kubernetes provides only basic audit capabilities, it should be enabled. You can enable it by setting an appropriate audit log path.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the master node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --audit-log-path argument is set as appropriate.
input_row.remediation: Edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the master node and set the --audit-log-path parameter to a suitable path and file where you would like audit logs to be written, for example: --audit-log-path=/var/log/apiserver/audit.log
input_row.default_value: By default, auditing is not enabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control requires checking the `--audit-log-path` argument for the Kubernetes API server, which is a standard configuration that can be queried via Kubernetes API or kubectl.
- The audit command provided in the control can be translated into a check for the API server configuration, which is accessible through the Kubernetes API.
- This control does not require subjective review or non-API host access.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_audit_log_path_set

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the following command to retrieve the API server configuration:
  ```bash
  kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].command}'
  ```
- This command will output the command-line arguments used to start the kube-apiserver, including the `--audit-log-path` argument.
- The expected configuration should include the `--audit-log-path` set to a valid file path (e.g., `/var/log/apiserver/audit.log`).
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** The output includes `--audit-log-path=<valid_path>`.
  - **Fail:** The output does not include `--audit-log-path` or it is set to an invalid path or not set at all.
